exemplar.title: To what extent does Netflix need to revise its strategic approach to overcome competition and increase market share in India?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does Netflix need to revise its strategic approach to overcome competition and increase market share in India?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear and consistent definition of the key concept âchangeâ
Repeated, explicit links to Netflix Indiaâs pricing, password-sharing and localisation strategies
Integration of âchangeâ across major analytical tools (STEEPLE, Ansoff, market-share)
weaknesses: Lacks deeper theoretical grounding (e.g., Kotter or Lewin change models)
Occasional generic statements about OTT platforms not fully tied back to Netflix
Limited interrogation of the concept at a strategic or theoretical level
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates a strong understanding of âchangeâ with frequent, well-placed references across analyses, fitting band 4. It stops short of band 5 because it doesnât deploy formal change theories or sustain the concept at a highly sophisticated level throughout every section.

strengths: Five supporting documents appropriately chosen to cover quantitative data and qualitative context
All sources clearly relevant to the Netflix India case
weaknesses: Depth of sources is uneven; several are journalistic pieces lacking methodological rigour
Range of perspectives is narrow (no triangulation with primary research or financial statements)
Inconsistent table formatting and incomplete entries reduce clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the quantity and relevance meet the upper bands, the uneven depth of analysis, narrow range of ideas and presentation flaws align more closely with band 2. For a band 3, sources would require more methodological rigour and broader triangulation.

strengths: Selection of STEEPLE, Ansoff Matrix and market-share tools directly addresses the research question
Each tool is applied to Netflixâs Indian context
weaknesses: Omission of the Ansoff Matrix from the methodology table and incomplete STEEPLE categories (e.g., environmental, technological)
Applications tend toward description rather than prioritised or quantified analysis
Lacks complementary tools (e.g., SWOT) to deepen insights
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The toolkit is adequate and relevant, but gaps in methodological clarity and a predominantly descriptive approach restrain it to band 2. A fully clear and effective application across all tools would merit band 3 or 4.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Incorporates multiple charts (market-share, pricing, content mix) and revenue data
Moves beyond description to evaluate pricing policy, password-sharing risks and localisation trade-offs
Links analysis back to the key concept of change
weaknesses: Integration of data is uneven; key metrics (profitability, subscriber growth, ROI timeframes) are omitted
Limited quantitative weighting or feasibility/timeline discussion for recommendations
Analysis sometimes lacks sustained depth and prioritisation of strategic options
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis is coherent and connects data to strategic implications, fitting band 3 (âsome analysis of the research questionâ). It falls short of band 4âs mostly effective, integrated evaluation because of missing quantitative rigor and uneven integration.

strengths: Conclusions are fully consistent with the evidence presented
Explicitly and directly answers the research question with clear summary of recommendations
weaknesses: Could be more concise and better prioritize recommendations
Lacks suggested timelines or sequencing to guide implementation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusions align perfectly with the findings and answer the research question, fulfilling band 3. They could be improved with greater concision and implementation detail, but they meet the maximum for this criterion.

strengths: Follows the prescribed IB structure with a clear title page, contents and labelled sections
Logical progression guides the reader effectively
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The organization is appropriate and consistent throughout, meeting all requirements for band 2.

strengths: All required presentation elements are included (word count, bibliography, page numbers, title page)
weaknesses: Figures and tables lack some captions, legends and formatting consistency
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although minor stylistic issues remain, the submission fulfills all core presentation requirements, fitting band 2.

strandSelections.reasoning: The IA follows the prescribed IB structure with a title page, contents, labelled sections, and logical progression, satisfying the description of an âappropriate structure throughoutâ (band 2).

**Strengths:** All documents are clearly relevant (industry censorship article, Statista revenue data, market-share chart, pricing comparison, investigative article). They provide both quantitative data and qualitative context.

**Weaknesses:** Depth is uneven; several sources are journalistic pieces with limited methodological rigour, and there is little triangulation or contrasting viewpoints. The range of ideas (e.g., primary research, financial statements) is narrow.

**Justification:** This matches band 3 â âdocuments are relevant and sufficiently in-depth,â but not band 4, which requires a broader range and greater depth.
All required presentation elementsâword count, bibliography, title page, table of contents and page numbersâare present. Hence the work attains the maximum band 2 for presentation.
Exactly five supporting documents are listed in Table 2, meeting the HL expectation for 3-5 sources. This quantity squarely fits the top numerical band.

**Application strengths:** Each tool is applied directly to Netflix (e.g., STEEPLE identifies censorship, Ansoff recommends rural bundling). The links to the research question are generally clear.

**Limitations:** Analysis sometimes becomes descriptive (listing factors or recommendations) without quantifying impact or prioritising options, so relevance is evident but not always rigorous.

**Justification:** Meets band 3 â ârelevance not always clear but adequately applied.â It does more than superficial application (band 2) yet lacks the clarity and effectiveness required for band 4.

Multiple graphs (market-share pie chart, pricing bar chart, content mix pie chart) and revenue figures are incorporated and referenced within the text. Data are generally appropriate and varied, demonstrating âsufficient selection and useâ characteristic of band 4. Some data gaps remain (e.g., profitability, subscriber growth), preventing the leap to exemplary band 5.

**Strengths:** The discussion moves beyond descriptionâevaluating pricing policy, password-sharing, and localisation trade-offs, and weighing tool limitations. Ideas are linked to the concept of change and to strategic implications.

**Weaknesses:** The evaluation is uneven; there is limited quantitative weighting of alternatives and little mention of timeframe or feasibility, so integration is present but not sustained.

**Justification:** This aligns with band 4 (âmostly effective analysis with some integrationâ) rather than band 5, which would expect sustained, in-depth integration.
The research question is explicitly answered (âNetflix must undertake significant change in pricing, localisation and market developmentâ). This direct response matches band 3.

The student chooses STEEPLE, Ansoff Matrix and Market-Share analysis, all appropriate to the research question. This constitutes an adequate, but not especially wide or innovative, toolkit; therefore band 3 (âadequate selectionâ) is most fitting.

The concluding section restates key findings (pricing, local content, rural expansion) and aligns them with evidence cited in earlier sections, showing consistency. Therefore band 3 (âconclusions fully consistent with evidenceâ) is appropriate.

**Strengths:** The investigation focuses exclusively on Netflix India; every data set, recommendation and evaluative comment ties directly to the organisationâs Indian operations. Competitive pricing, local content deals and regulatory pressures are analysed with clear reference to company policies and performance data.

**Weaknesses:** Integration is occasionally interrupted by generic statements about âOTT platformsâ without tying back to Netflix, and there is little discussion of Netflixâs internal capabilities or culture, so the link, while strong, is not entirely seamless.

**Justification:** The quality fits band 4 (âstrong analysis with partial integrationâ) rather than band 5, which would require a fully interwoven organisational focus with no lapses.

**Strengths:** The student demonstrates a solid grasp of the key concept of change; its definition is stated and repeatedly linked to pricing, password-sharing and content localisation. In every major analytical section (âSTEEPLEâ, âAnsoffâ, âMarket Shareâ) the narrative explicitly returns to âembracing changeâ or âmanaging this changeâ, showing more than a token reference.

**Weaknesses:** The concept is not interrogated at a strategic or theoretical level (e.g., Lewin/Kotter models are absent), so the integration, while frequent, is still partial rather than fully woven into every layer of analysis.

**Justification:** This aligns with band 4 â âstrong understanding with partial integration throughout assessment.â It surpasses band 3 because the concept is employed in several analytical tools, but falls short of band 5 where integration would be sustained and sophisticated throughout.

annotations.comment: Quotation from âKaurâ lacks full bibliographic detail; ensure in-text author-date citations align with the bibliography.
Figure 1.1 is presented without caption or axis labels; add descriptive captions and clear axis indicators to enhance interpretability.
The Ansoff Growth Matrix is appropriately introduced; consider citing the source or data basis used to position Netflix in each quadrant.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The Ansoff matrix figure lacks a legend explaining how Netflixâs strategies map to quadrants; annotate the diagram with key strategic insights.
The market-penetration discussion on password sharing is insightful; strengthen it by incorporating data on sharing-account prevalence or revenue impact.
The narrative effectively emphasizes change, but integrating consumer-adoption theory (e.g., Rogersâ Diffusion) would deepen the analysis.
Proposed local-content partnerships are promising, but lack of timelines or resource estimates weakens feasibility; include a basic feasibility analysis.
The Market Development strategy is pertinent, but the link to Netflixâs internal capabilities is underdeveloped; assess resource strengths for partnerships.
To substantiate rural-expansion recommendations, include a brief SWOT or resource audit to evaluate Netflixâs capacity for telecom partnerships.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Good acknowledgement of Ansoff Matrix limitations; consider complementing with a SWOT analysis for more balanced insight.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Introduce the market-share analysis with explicit date and source citations here to anchor the reader in context.
The pie chart is visually clear but lacks percentage labels on each slice; add data labels for immediate reader comprehension.
The research question is clearly articulated and focused, providing a strong foundation for the investigationâs structure and scope.
The key concept of change is defined well, but the analysis would benefit from integration of strategic change theories (e.g., Kotterâs 8-Step Model) to deepen conceptual rigor.
The table of contents is comprehensive with accurate section and page references, supporting clear navigation of the assessment.
Ensure consistency between the table of contents and actual section headings; some section titles vary slightly in wording.
The methodology table omits the Ansoff Growth Matrix, which is used later; include all selected tools here for methodological clarity.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Good definition of change; however, applying a formal change model (e.g., Lewinâs UnfreezeâChangeâRefreeze) would strengthen theoretical integration.
Table formatting for the Ansoff matrix is unclear and contains stray OCR math; refine layout and correct errors to improve readability.
The supporting-documents table contains empty cells and inconsistent row formatting; ensure each entry is complete and consistently presented.
The STEEPLE introduction is descriptive; to enhance analysis, explicitly link each external factor back to the research question.
In-text citations (e.g., SD1, SD5) are inconsistent; adopt a standard referencing style (author-date) to improve academic rigor.
STEEPLE analysis omits several categories (e.g., Technological, Environmental); include all seven factors for a comprehensive framework.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Good reflection on the limitations of STEEPLE, but reinforce this by linking back to strategic recommendations and the key concept of change.
The bibliography includes all sources but citation styles vary; standardize to one format (e.g., APA) throughout to improve presentation.
Inline math tags are misused; format equations using $$ â¦ $$ for LaTeX consistency and readability.
The content-mix pie chart lacks a title and data-source caption; add a descriptive caption and reference the supporting document.
Investment figures (e.g., â¹3,000 Cr) need context regarding time frame and expected ROI to support evaluative claims.
Conclusions effectively summarize findings but could be more concise and prioritize key recommendations with timelines.
